key_insight: The paper uses proximal causal inference to identify mediation path-specific effects even when treatment-induced recanting witnesses are hidden or only partially observable.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical, statistically robust way to estimate direct and indirect causal pathways in real systems where key confounders are missing, sensitive, or hard to measure.
method: Derive three identification strategies for path-specific effects with unknown recanting witnesses.
Construct the efficient influence function and a proximal multiply robust estimator.
Use debiased machine learning with minimax optimization for point estimates and confidence intervals.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports simulation studies and a real data application to demonstrate performance, along with semiparametric efficiency claims under correct nuisance specification.
watch_item: Valid inference still depends on suitable proxy variables and at least one correctly specified nuisance model, so proxy quality and model choice are critical.
paper_title: Proximal Mediation Analysis with Hidden Recanting Witnesses
